goal: Add 7 high-impact features across 3 utilities: cut -f field mode, sort -k/-t/-u, grep -n/-l/-w.

features.title: cut: -f field mode
features.instructions:
Implement -f field mode in Lentils/Cut/Logic.lean. Currently -c (character mode) works but -f is a stub.

1. In parseArgs, keep existing -f recognition. The Config already has Mode.fields.

2. In processInput (or the per-line processing function), when mode is Mode.fields:
   - Split each line by the delimiter (cfg.delim).
   - Use parseRangeList to select fields by number (1-indexed, same as cut).
   - Rejoin selected fields with the delimiter.
   - If -s (suppress) is set and the line has no delimiter, skip it (already implemented).

3. The field extraction should match POSIX behavior:
   - Fields are 1-indexed.
   - No delimiter in line → output depends on -s flag (without -s, pass through; with -s, skip).
   - Ranges like -f1,3-5,7 work as expected.

4. Test: echo 'a:b:c' | lentils cut -d: -f2 → 'b'. echo 'a:b:c:d:e' | lentils cut -d: -f1,3-4 → 'a:c:d'. echo 'no_delim' | lentils cut -d: -f1 → 'no_delim'. echo 'no_delim' | lentils cut -d: -s -f1 → ''.

File: Lentils/Cut/Logic.lean
Validation: lake build, manual tests above

features.title: sort: -k -t -u key/field/unique
features.instructions:
Add -k (key), -t (field separator), and -u (unique) to Lentils/Sort/Logic.lean.

1. Update parseArgs to recognize:
   - -t char → set field separator
   - -k keydef → set sort key (format: startField[.startChar][,endField[.endChar]][type])
   - -u → unique mode (suppress duplicate lines)

2. For -k keydef: parse as "F[.C][,F[.C]][flags]". For simplicity, support:
   - "-k2" → sort by field 2
   - "-k2,2" → sort by field 2 only (stable within field 2)
   - "-k2n" → numeric sort by field 2
   - "-k2,2n" → numeric sort by field 2

3. For -t char: split each line by the separator, extract key fields, compare those first, then fall back to whole-line comparison for ties.

4. For -u: after sorting, remove adjacent duplicate lines (same as uniq after sort). Lines are duplicates if they compare equal.

5. Update sortLines to accept the new parameters. The insertionSort function should be extended to support key extraction.

6. Tests:
   - printf 'b:2\na:1\nc:3\n' | lentils sort -t: -k2n → 'a:1\nb:2\nc:3\n'
   - printf 'a\nb\na\n' | lentils sort -u → 'a\nb\n'
   - printf 'z:1\na:2\na:1\n' | lentils sort -t: -k2 → 'a:1\nz:1\na:2\n'

File: Lentils/Sort/Logic.lean, Lentils/Sort/Sort.lean
Validation: lake build, manual tests above

features.title: grep: -n -l -w flags
features.instructions:
Add -n (line number), -l (files with matches), and -w (word match) to grep.

1. -n (line number): Add line number prefix to output. In processInput, when lineNumber flag is true, prefix each matching line with "{lineno}:". Update run to not add line numbers in count or quiet mode.

2. -l (files with matches): Print only filenames that contain a match. In run (Grep.lean), when flag.l is true: process input as usual to determine if any match exists; if so, print the filename (or "(standard input)" for stdin) and exit 0. Stop processing after first match.

3. -w (word match): Match whole words only. A word is a sequence of alphanumeric characters bounded by non-alphanumeric or line boundaries. In containsPattern/lineMatches, after finding a match at position p with length n, check that (p==0 or non-word char before) and (p+n==text.size or non-word char after).

4. Add flags to Flags structure and parseArgs recognition.

5. Tests:
   - printf 'hello\nworld\n' | lentils grep -n hello → '1:hello'
   - echo 'hello' > /tmp/greptest && lentils grep -l hello /tmp/greptest → '/tmp/greptest'
   - printf 'hello\nthe shell\na hello world\n' | lentils grep -w hello → 'hello\n' (not 'a hello world')

File: Lentils/Grep/Logic.lean, Lentils/Grep/Grep.lean
validation:
printf 'hello
shell
a hello world
' | lentils grep -w hello → 'hello'
